task_title: Remove orphaned states and dead code after UX flow changes
context: After removing TakeAway-per-item flow and adding split dialog, orphaned code remained in Menu.tsx and Cart.tsx that was never cleaned up.

Menu.tsx: Removed function: performAddDirectly (dead code — TakeAwayModal removed from render)
Removed component: TakeAwayModal (defined but never rendered)
Cleaned: handleAddToCart — removed pendingTakeAwayType injection logic
Cart.tsx: Removed imports: DollarSign, LayoutGrid, List, ArrowRight, Share2

notes: All orphaned code was a result of the UX flow change from TakeAway-per-item to TakeAway-at-checkout. React had no runtime errors but TS could have caught these at compile time. Lesson: run audit after every major flow change.
patterns_discovered: When removing a multi-step modal flow, always grep for all state variables that fed that flow
Orphaned states from removed modal flows appear in useEffect deps — check those too
